title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a behavior-and-type regression suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"5" that must not enter the trusted model.

Inspect the Behavior Type Tests trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Type Complexity Restraint: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"3" that must not enter the trusted model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.
